key_insight: OrchRM learns a reward model for multi-agent orchestration directly from intermediate execution artifacts, avoiding expensive human labels and sub-agent rollouts.
engineering_relevance: This provides a more scalable way to train and deploy LLM-based orchestrators for multi-agent systems in math, web QA, and multi-hop reasoning.
method: Build win-lose pairs from intermediate artifacts of multi-agent runs
Train a Bradley-Terry reward model at the orchestration level
Use the learned reward to guide orchestrator training and test-time scaling
evidence_signal: The abstract reports up to 10x lower token usage during training and up to 8% higher test-time scaling accuracy across multiple domains.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of automatically constructed preference pairs, so noisy artifacts could bias the reward model.
